answer:
Sketch:
- Acquired 2016 → 100% exclusion era (post-9/27/2010)
- Held >5 years ✓
- Cap: greater of $10M or 10× basis
- $8M < $10M cap
- Excluded gain also exempt from AMT & NIIT

Answer: Roughly $0 federal tax. Shares acquired in 2016 and held over five years qualify for the 100% Section 1202 exclusion, and your $8M gain sits under the $10M per-issuer cap. The excluded gain isn't an AMT preference item and escapes the 3.8% NIIT too. Just confirm the standard QSBS conditions held: original issuance (not secondary purchase), the company's gross assets were ≤$50M at issuance, it's a domestic C-corp in a qualified trade, and no disqualifying redemptions. Also check your state — some (notably California) don't conform and will tax the full gain.
